Manganaro, 1989, Iscrizioni Latine nuove e vecchie della Sicilia
Claudius or Nero, i.e. mid 1st century CE
'Decisamente di III sc.d.C.; possibly of Probus, cf. Dessau ILS 594)

To Severus, Caracalla and
                                Geta, so between 198 and 211
Latin text, at least one line, on one long face of the block.

The text is carved on the internal, roughly prepared surface, and therefore seemingly not visible within the construction; it is likely that there was more text preceding on this on an adjoining block

Remains of two lines, extending to right margin, with substantial space between (large enough for a short intervening line). A guideline is visible between the preserved lines, another parallel to the bottom edge 3.2 cm from the edge.

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag, edition source:
Dating to the third or fourth century CE on the basis of the formula
Remains of two lines of Latin letters, with a vacat above, and 1-2 letter vacat margin on the right; right margin not perfectly justified.
Neatly carved regular tall letters, relatively narrow module, with serifs. G has a detached and curved bar. No visible use of interpuncts.
